116916.explanation: Striving for an equal distribution is very noble, but a mandatory equal distribution does not seem desirable to us. The position must be held by competent people, regardless of gender.

116917.explanation: All topics are important, but not equally important to everyone. As soon as a topic directly impacts the daily lives of all residents, a referendum should be made discussable.

116922.explanation: This fully depends on the question. Saying no in advance is unHulsters; entrepreneurs must be able to do business. The priority should be filling vacancies such as the large DC on Hogeweg, Tieleman in Kloosterzande, and the former Pabo in Walsoorden.

116923.explanation: Our municipality is one of peace and safety. We do not want to jeopardize that by opening an asylum seeker center. The asylum issue must be addressed nationally and not left to small communities.

116924.explanation: Throughout the municipality, there is housing for guest workers, for which there are clear and strict rules. It may be solved on private property, but that is not an obligation and must comply with regulations.

116928.explanation: Once the fireworks ban takes effect, the municipality could organize a fireworks show in the various centers. This brings people together, keeps the tradition alive, and increases safety.

116931.explanation: If this applies, the municipality should offer a helping hand. Also in any permit process. In Hulst, we live together and everyone must be able to participate.

116932.explanation: This is already possible, but not in all cases, and that is also not desirable. With the right destination, a building permit, and a good plan, any farmer can already approach the municipality.

116934.explanation: The local broadcaster of Hulst has merged into a Zeeuws-Vlaamse station. It must therefore be a joint effort by Sluis, Terneuzen, and Hulst to support this broadcaster.

116935.explanation: Musical development is good for every child. But so are sports and other club activities. If a subsidy is set up for this, it should be comprehensive for all extracurricular activities.

116938.explanation: We would prefer to see more police presence on the streets. With fixed contact points in each center, bringing back the visible community officer.

116939.explanation: At problem locations, the step toward camera surveillance can definitely be reduced. But we must continue to guard the privacy of our residents.

116940.explanation: Two housing associations are active in our municipality. They have already run a pilot giving priority to returning students. This keeps our region young, vital, and creates opportunities for our residents.

116942.explanation: Holiday homes are for recreational use. The facilities are also tailored to this. Moreover, permanent residence often seems to be a breeding ground for criminal activities.

116943.explanation: It must be possible to build in every center, even if that means adding residences in rural areas. While taking into account the tranquility we cherish.

116944.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate in our municipality; there is a place for everyone and work for those who want to work. Both voluntary and paid.

116946.explanation: This is a national trend; more and more households with higher incomes struggle to make ends meet. This issue should be addressed at the national level.

116949.explanation: Speed limits on no road should be unnecessarily low. Speeds must fit the situation and traffic density.

116950.explanation: If people want to deviate from regular care, it is their own choice and responsibility. The municipality does not need to facilitate this.

116953.explanation: This is an outdated regulation that is not used for dogs. No poop bags are hung, no dog walking areas are created. It is a nice bonus on the general reserve at the expense of the dog owner.

116954.explanation: We are an attractive region for tourists; therefore, demand is high, and prices can certainly keep pace with inflation.

116955.explanation: Entrepreneurship must be stimulated. Hulst must also be a beautiful municipality to work in with a suitable job for everyone. The focus must first be on filling vacancies and existing sites.

116958.explanation: This fully depends on location and situation. Also, there is currently no capacity on the grid to connect large solar parks.

116959.explanation: This is an important facility for our residents; we must maintain it and expand it if desired.

116961.explanation: We live in the Netherlands, so we speak Dutch. Dialects are not something to be taught in schools.

116962.explanation: We want to maintain the zero-tolerance policy.

116968.explanation: We must not forget that residents also need to park. Although many spots are regularly empty at the ramparts, they can quickly fill up on a busy Sunday.

116970.explanation: We want to relieve Kuitaart, but a bypass through a nature area does not seem like the right option. Road users from Kloosterzande must keep a smooth connection towards the south.

116973.explanation: A good connection towards Brabant, but also towards the rest of Zeeland and the Netherlands is crucial. There must be no isolation if you do not own a car.

116916.explanation: Of course, we also want more women in leadership positions. We certainly see in Zeeland politics that the gender distribution is skewed. We encourage women to run and offer help and advice if they have questions. However, gender is only one factor of diversity.

116923.explanation: The municipality must comply with applicable laws and regulations and therefore Hulst will have to take its share under the distribution law. We do not choose a large asylum seeker center but prefer small-scale shelter that is not necessarily located in one place.

116951.explanation: A combination of the care to be provided and the price, with the care being the most important. This concerns vulnerable young people.

116955.explanation: If there is a demand for expansion, this must be assessed to see if it is possible.

116958.explanation: We want alternatives to be sought first where solar panels are installed on roofs.

116961.explanation: It is up to the schools themselves to give shape to this; of course, we favor the municipality helping find ways to pass on our culture and history to the next generation.

116916.explanation: Of course, it is important to have a good balance in representation between women and men. Knowledge and skills must weigh heavily.

116917.explanation: This only applies to major decisions where politics itself cannot reach a clear decision.

116922.explanation: There are enough of these centers available. First filling the centers that have already been realized.

116923.explanation: National regulations are so unclear and the societal debate is large. GHH believes that real refugees should be helped and that Hulst has a role in this. Within current law, a location with flexible housing has been chosen. We prefer small-scale shelter.

116924.explanation: Housing must be adequate. This may be at companies or in small-scale facilities outside the companies.

116928.explanation: Fireworks are a tradition. The national ban will be difficult to enforce. So this seems to us a good alternative.

116931.explanation: Accessibility is a great value. Especially in public buildings, this must be the standard. It should not be mandatory for every business.

116932.explanation: Municipalities must regulate this. No uncontrolled construction in the polder.

116934.explanation: We have already reached agreement on this in Zeeuws-Vlaanderen. Additional funds must come from the national government, as has also been promised.

116938.explanation: Buitengewoon opsporingsambtenaren (BOAs) alone cannot guarantee safety. We remain of the opinion that sufficient police deployment is necessary.

116939.explanation: Where there is nuisance, this must be possible.

116940.explanation: Among our residents, especially young people, the demand for housing is great. They want to continue living and working in their own residential area.

116942.explanation: For livability, as much occupancy as possible is desirable.

116943.explanation: Building where needed can also be outside town centers. For example, Waterzande.

116944.explanation: For those who can, this is a nice alternative to regular employment.

116946.explanation: For those who need help, it must be available. Participation in society through work or volunteer work should be the goal. Municipalities must pay attention to the poverty trap when setting income limits.

116949.explanation: There are many complaints about speed within built-up areas. 30 km per hour can reduce this.

116950.explanation: Care provision is already tight. If this is possible, fine. But freedom of choice is limited by availability.

116953.explanation: This is an “old” tax. Municipalities should have more possibilities to levy purpose-specific taxes. Abolishment must not be passed on to homeowners via property tax (ozb).

116954.explanation: Tourist tax is relatively low. Collection costs must not exceed the amount collected.

116955.explanation: Currently, no locations are available anymore. Business activity must remain possible.

116958.explanation: Not in a general sense. Only on a smaller local scale, directly at companies.

116959.explanation: An alternative to the current swimming pool must remain within the municipality’s current financial possibilities.

116961.explanation: We consider dialects a cultural heritage. It is a shame that they are disappearing more and more. We have many dialects, but we see no role for schools in this.

116962.explanation: As long as no other national policy comes, this is asking for nuisance.

116968.explanation: There must remain sufficient space. There are fewer stalls on the market, but according to counts there remains sufficient opportunity.

116970.explanation: A nice wish, but very difficult to realize.

116973.explanation: The alternative is working well and must be preserved.

116916.explanation: That should always be the goal.

116917.explanation: There are already enough channels and initiatives to express your opinion, such as Doemee.

116922.explanation: Such a center brings little to Hulst, and mainly has negative consequences for traffic and the environment.

116923.explanation: At least the currently intended new refugee shelter.

116924.explanation: They must not be too dependent on their employer, and the employer must not be overburdened with such demands.

116931.explanation: The municipality may also inform entrepreneurs that it can offer commercial opportunities.

116934.explanation: Provided the distribution with other Zeeuws-Vlaamse municipalities remains fair and it provides a quality boost.

116935.explanation: Music is good for the social and intellectual development of every child.

116938.explanation: The current situation does not call for more.

116940.explanation: That will be very difficult to enforce, and moreover we should welcome all new residents.

116942.explanation: Everyone with a second home has sufficient means to make a contribution for this.

116946.explanation: There are many arrangements in this area to accommodate it.

116949.explanation: Where possible and where safety is increased.

116950.explanation: There is already a shortage of healthcare providers; all hands are welcome.

116953.explanation: If the dog tax is maintained, the funds must be specifically spent on dog-related facilities.

116954.explanation: In many municipalities with fewer tourist attractions, this tax is already much higher.

116955.explanation: Aim to strengthen local entrepreneurs and innovative companies.

116958.explanation: If there are initiatives for it, they should certainly be supported.

116959.explanation: The municipality must continue to financially support the swimming pool.

116961.explanation: There should be attention to local culture and history at school.

116968.explanation: More space for staying and for pedestrians, cyclists, and cultural and shopping public.

116970.explanation: At least an acceptable traffic solution.

116916.explanation: There must be strong and good governance.

116917.explanation: The council is there to make choices; if everything could be decided by referendum, we could abolish everything. Most importantly, referendums can be manipulated, as seen with BREXIT.

116922.explanation: There are already too many in Hulst! These should be more by highways, not in an area like Hulst.

116923.explanation: There should be no asylum seekers' center, but a reception center is acceptable if coordinated with all municipalities; otherwise not.

116924.explanation: Preferably not, but if it really cannot be avoided, there must be an option where everything is well arranged for the people.

116928.explanation: There is no budget for this at the municipality.

116931.explanation: It is advised to do this everywhere and it is a requirement to make it accessible. You would then have to financially support everyone as a municipality, and that is not the intention.

116932.explanation: Yes, this is possible but only in consultation and some rules have been established in our beautiful municipality because it must fit within Spatial Planning.

116934.explanation: Yes, we have been doing this as the municipality of Hulst for years.

116935.explanation: Yes, we are still one of the few municipalities in Zeeland with our own music school, which we financially support well as a municipality.

116938.explanation: But then really focused on safety and not on parking.

116939.explanation: It already exists and can certainly be expanded.

116942.explanation: Yes, but limited and not on a large scale; in our municipality, we do not have as much trouble with this as other coastal municipalities. Livability comes first.

116943.explanation: As a municipality, we build neighborhoods ourselves as new developments outside the village boundaries.

116944.explanation: It is important to activate people to work.

116946.explanation: The border in Hulst has been set by the municipal council.

116949.explanation: Many through roads within built-up areas are perfectly designed for 50 km and should remain at 50 km. Design is crucial.

116950.explanation: We offer what is possible and customized solutions, as this cannot be realized in our municipality as it is in the Randstad.

116953.explanation: It would be allowed, but we currently use this money to create dog parks here and there with costs for maintenance.

116954.explanation: It really should not go up, but it is normal throughout Europe to pay this. There should actually be a nationwide rate.

116958.explanation: Yes, it is also possible, but we have the problem that there are few connection points to the grid where this could be done.

116959.explanation: Not just giving money; there must be something in return, and there may be market parties willing to do this as well.

116961.explanation: Not really necessary; it is 2026, we don't live in Friesland.

116962.explanation: We have been saying no in Hulst for years; the municipality of Terneuzen has had issues for years with French...

116968.explanation: No, actually more but then smarter, better utilizing strongholds, etc....

116970.explanation: Yes, it is an important point in our election program.

116973.explanation: Indeed! And not just driving to Roosendaal but from Roosendaal to Breda. The train between Roosendaal and Breda is also always important for our students.

116916.explanation: Governing is done together! D66 Hulst wants a college that reflects our residents. An equal distribution of men and women ensures better plans and broader support. We choose quality and diversity at the governance table. It is possible: an inclusive Hulst!

116917.explanation: D66 Hulst stands for open governance. We want to strengthen democracy by giving residents more direct influence. A referendum for major decisions is a powerful tool to test support. This way, politics decides not over people, but with the people. It is possible!

116922.explanation: First, realize an extra connection to Belgium. We have limited space and distribution centers add very little. Preferably innovative and sustainable companies on the scarce business land we have.

116923.explanation: Everyone deserves a safe home. D66 Hulst takes responsibility for humane, small-scale shelter. No megahalls, but locations that fit our neighborhood, such as at the Spoorweg. From day one, we focus on participation, language lessons, and work. It is possible: humane and realistic sheltering!

116924.explanation: Labor migrants are indispensable for our farmers. D66 Hulst wants to allow housing on their own premises to reduce pressure on residential areas. However, we demand decent accommodations, good employment practices, and safety supervision. No exploitation, but decent shelter. It is possible!

116928.explanation: New Year's Eve must be a celebration for everyone. D66 Hulst chooses protection over nuisance. We want a professional, central fireworks or laser show as a safe new tradition. This way we celebrate together without damage, animal suffering, or danger for emergency responders. It is possible!

116931.explanation: All public buildings must be equally accessible to everyone. Entrepreneurs have their own responsibility here, and building codes impose requirements. We must pay extra attention here because it is not well organized everywhere.

116932.explanation: Building on the farm? Yes, if it helps to counteract vacancies! D66 Hulst supports converting old stables into homes. This offers farmers perspective and starters a chance, without filling our beautiful polders with random new construction. It is possible: vital and livable countryside!

116934.explanation: A strong democracy cannot exist without critical journalism. D66 Hulst wants to invest in the local broadcaster so they can independently fulfill their monitoring role. This prevents disinformation and strengthens free debate in our 14 communities. It is possible: an open and honest Hulst!

116935.explanation: Music lessons should not be a luxury. D66 Hulst wants every child to have the chance to discover talent, regardless of their parents' wallet. Through the 'Rich School Day' we bring music into the classroom and support local associations. Equal opportunities start with culture for everyone. It is possible!

116938.explanation: Safety through proximity! D66 Hulst wants more enforcement officers visible in the neighborhood, not just to issue fines but to safeguard livability and be approachable. We choose a smart combination of enforcement, prevention, and strong neighborhood officers. It is possible!

116939.explanation: In the city center, provided privacy is guaranteed, and no more Chinese junk like previously purchased. So only with good safeguards.

116940.explanation: If that is legally possible, that would be nice. Same with new construction: give local residents the first chance to register for homes.

116942.explanation: D66 Hulst wants to prevent vacancies and decay. We are open to creative housing forms. Recreational use of homes can be a temporary solution to the housing shortage, provided the quality is good and it fits the livability.

116943.explanation: D66 Hulst prioritizes infill first: splitting vacant buildings and converting shops. But the housing shortage is too big to hermetically close village boundaries. We also build outside the border where necessary, provided it is green, sustainable, and for our own starters and elderly. It is possible!

116944.explanation: D66 Hulst chooses trust, not coercion. A mandatory counterperformance does not help people find jobs faster. We want to encourage residents with tailored support and education. The government should stand next to people, not act as a controller.

116946.explanation: Existential security is the foundation for freedom. D66 Hulst wants help to be accessible for everyone who really needs it, including those just above minimum income. By raising the income limit, we prevent people from falling between the cracks. It is possible: a social Hulst!

116949.explanation: Safety first! D66 Hulst wants 30 km/h to be the norm in our residential areas and villages. This saves lives and makes the street a place for children to play and neighbors to meet. Only on major through roads will 50 km/h remain possible. It is possible!

116950.explanation: D66 stands for self-direction and inclusivity. Good care is personal; therefore it must align with one's identity and values. We choose care that truly understands the patient, regardless of culture or belief. Freedom is being able to choose.

116951.explanation: Quality is non-negotiable when it comes to our children. D66 Hulst chooses care that works, not the lowest price. We invest in effective help and prevention so children get better and faster support. Health is not a market product but a basic right.

116953.explanation: Away with the dog tax! D66 Hulst finds this fee outdated and illogical. Dogs provide companionship and exercise; we should not punish that with taxes. We choose a fair and transparent tax system without unnecessary checks behind the door. It is possible!

116954.explanation: The tourist enjoys it, the tourist pays for it. D66 Hulst wants to fairly price the tourist tax to invest in our museums, bike paths, and the Zeeland coast. This way we improve tourism quality without extra burden on our local residents. It is possible: a balance between peace and tourism!

116955.explanation: No more big box shifters in Hulst. D66 Hulst chooses a smart economy within the limits of our landscape. We do not want unnecessary green expansion. Companies that work circularly and innovatively get space with us. Quality over quantity!

116958.explanation: Roofs first! D66 Hulst does not want to sacrifice valuable farmland if the roofs of sheds and stables are still unused. We follow the solar ladder: first roofs, then parking lots and verges. We only make exceptions for smart combinations of agriculture and energy generation. It is possible!

116959.explanation: Sports and swimming lessons must remain accessible for everyone in Hulst. D66 Hulst supports keeping a swimming pool but demands full transparency about the costs. No more blank checks: we choose a sustainable, modern pool that fits our ambitions. It is possible: swimming in Hulst!

116961.explanation: Language is identity. D66 Hulst wants schools to have space to pay attention to 'Het Hulsters' as cultural heritage. It strengthens the bond between young and old and pride in our region. Dialect alongside good Dutch: it is possible! A rich culture begins in the classroom.

116962.explanation: Regulating is safer than banning. D66 Hulst wants to get rid of the 'zero option' that encourages street trade and crime. A legal coffeeshop ensures controlled quality, better information, and less street nuisance. We choose a realistic policy. It is possible!

116968.explanation: The car is a guest. D66 Hulst wants to exchange street parking for more greenery, terraces, and space for cyclists. By parking cars smartly underground (parking garage!) or on the city outskirts, we make the Grote Markt the living room of the city again. Quality over metal! It is possible!

116970.explanation: D66 Hulst chooses a safe rural area without unnecessary new asphalt. We want to improve traffic safety on existing routes first and discourage rat running. Only if safety in the core really cannot be improved otherwise do we consider alternatives. These exist in Kuitaart.

116973.explanation: Accessibility is freedom. D66 Hulst demands better public transport to Breda and Antwerp. We do not want to go back to slow routes from the past but fight for fast, borderless connections. Whether bus or train: our students and workers deserve robust network connections.

116917.explanation: Every resident has the right to propose a referendum.

116922.explanation: Encourage innovation towards the future and expansion if desired. Businesses are important for a municipality.

116923.explanation: The residents of the municipality of Hulst are against it and afraid of the consequences, given that crime is increasing in other places.

116924.explanation: It would be better for the workers but with adapted accommodations and under municipal supervision.

116928.explanation: Why? Then all residents from the villages would have to go to Hulst.

116931.explanation: All public buildings should be accessible to people with disabilities.

116932.explanation: Provided it is placed only on farmland and follows the proper procedures and permits.

116934.explanation: The broadcaster has merged with the local broadcaster in ZVL and should definitely be subsidized.

116935.explanation: Totally agree, music is important for children. An ICO should also be part of this.

116938.explanation: Especially in the villages of the municipality of Hulst.

116939.explanation: For better control of traffic and citizens, this is safe, monitored 24 hours and connected to the control room.

116940.explanation: Certainly, local residents who are on the waiting list take priority.

116942.explanation: For people waiting for a home or temporary accommodation.

116943.explanation: Where the villages belong, these are included as well.

116944.explanation: The municipality has determined who receives social assistance benefits and who is obliged to apply for jobs.

116946.explanation: The municipality must assist in preventing poverty.

116949.explanation: 30 km/h speed limits only within residential neighborhoods and the city center.

116950.explanation: This seems like discrimination; a person is a person, and the fact that their faith differs from a healthcare provider should not matter in treatment.

116953.explanation: None of the proceeds go to dog owners. Outside the city, Hulst has a dog park, but unfortunately it is too far for village residents who would want one in their village as well.

116954.explanation: Would not recommend keeping the tourist tax as is, given that VAT on tourism has already been increased.

116955.explanation: In view of the future, it should also apply to the villages.

116958.explanation: Already implemented in many municipalities, with consultation of residents. Open days and discussions are held. They can also be placed at height with sheep grazing underneath.

116959.explanation: Rightly stop paying out 300,000 and invest that money in an outdoor pool with all facilities.

116961.explanation: At primary schools, Standard Dutch (ABN) should be taught, and at home parents can use the dialect.

116962.explanation: This would improve the legality of drugs and reduce crime-related problems.

116968.explanation: Due to expansions in the city center, more cars will come. So expanding an underground parking garage under the market is already a solution.

116970.explanation: This should apply to many villages.

116973.explanation: Definitely, because this is for the young people who go to school there. A train connection would also be a good future plan; there is already a connection from Breda to the border.

116916.explanation: Based on quality. Half of the population is female.

116917.explanation: The residents of Hulst feel unheard on many issues. They should also be able to make their voices heard between elections.

116922.explanation: It is a disgrace that such a huge empty building stands in Hulst. This should never have happened. The road network is not prepared for it, it creates no jobs for residents of Hulst. The SP believes that the empty mega distribution center should be converted into a logistics training center.

116923.explanation: The SP believes it should be small-scale only and not like the COA currently operates. Reception locations should be spread across all villages in the municipality.

116924.explanation: Employers are responsible, if they bring people here, for decent housing, but it must never be tied to employment. We mean that if people lose their jobs, they should not immediately become homeless, as is currently the case.

116928.explanation: The municipality should consider offering an alternative for the youth.

116931.explanation: Not in all cases, and only if the entrepreneur can demonstrate that they are financially unable to do so themselves.

116932.explanation: Building on a farm is only allowed within existing rules and laws. It is currently well regulated. However, consideration should be given to redeveloping old, vacant farms.

116934.explanation: But in Hulst, extra attention must be paid to the political independence of those involved in the local broadcaster.

116935.explanation: Music lessons are not a luxury but a right. Children with wealthy parents can now attend music school, but children from poorer families cannot. That is unfair. Music helps children develop, do well at school, build confidence, and be happy.

116938.explanation: Safety is explicitly a task of the police.

116939.explanation: Cameras create a false sense of security. Real security comes from community police officers who know the residents, from good youth work, from lively neighborhoods where people look out for each other. Cameras are symptom treatment-they solve nothing, they just shift problems.

116940.explanation: The SP believes that Hulst should prioritize building affordable housing for young and old. Hulst has failed for decades to meet the national 30% social housing standard; that percentage has only decreased. We believe too many expensive houses are being built.

116942.explanation: But we must prevent homes intended for residence from being sold as holiday homes. The municipality must invest in truly affordable housing.

116943.explanation: Peace and space are one of our gems. In some cases, it is possible to build affordable additional houses adjacent to an existing village.

116944.explanation: If people do meaningful work, they should be paid for it and not need benefits.

116946.explanation: Hulst has been performing poorly for years compared to other municipalities. It would be even better if poverty policy were regulated nationally.

116949.explanation: That must be enforced, however. There is already enough cardboard-box legislation.

116950.explanation: We believe that all facilities should be public. And not for a specific target group.

116951.explanation: Hulst has made a mess of youth care, precisely because it made wrong financial choices. Children who need care must be able to count on the best available care. We also believe that youth care should be regulated provincially again.

116953.explanation: The SP believes that collecting this tax costs more than it yields.

116954.explanation: Tourists who use our facilities can certainly pay a bit more for it.

116955.explanation: We think that within existing industrial areas there is enough space to allow new initiatives. However, we want existing industrial areas to be greener.

116958.explanation: As far as the SP is concerned, the municipality must do everything possible to make maximum use of the roofs of homes, business premises, schools, sports halls, and municipal buildings for solar panels. Solar panels belong on roofs, not on the ground.

116959.explanation: We would very much like that, but unfortunately it is legally not possible. We believe that Hulst should invest in a new public swimming pool that meets the standards of today.

116961.explanation: Regional language is heritage, part of communities, and must not disappear.

116962.explanation: We think it is very naive to expect that illegal drug trade does not take place if you live in a border municipality. Regulation is better than denial. A coffeeshop may not be near a school.

116968.explanation: There are already insufficient parking spaces for residents. In new construction, the project developer must ensure there are enough parking spaces on private property.

116970.explanation: It is the only way to keep Kuitaart livable in the long term. Research must already take place now on how this can be achieved while preserving existing nature areas.

116973.explanation: The Roosendaal bus is fine. However, there must be a direct public transport connection to Antwerp.

116916.explanation: It's about quality; we don't care whether it is a man or a woman, but the best person in the right position.

116917.explanation: We want more influence from citizens, but this does not necessarily have to be through a referendum.

116922.explanation: The municipality of Hulst is too small for this form of industry.

116923.explanation: There is too little support among our residents for this. Our municipality is also not sufficiently suitable to offer these people a proper quality of life. Financially, it is not feasible for Hulst.

116924.explanation: These people must be housed in the municipality; this does not have to be solely on the industrial estate.

116928.explanation: If possible, even in multiple villages, possibly in consultation with local associations; this will contribute to enforcing/maintaining the fireworks ban.

116931.explanation: Entrepreneurs are themselves responsible for the accessibility of their building to people with disabilities. The municipality must also make public spaces accessible in this regard.

116932.explanation: We have an enormous housing shortage, but we must also be careful that the entire polder is not built over. After weighing interests, it must sometimes be possible.

116934.explanation: It is important to preserve this broadcaster, but coverage must always remain independent and of good quality.

116935.explanation: There is already an existing arrangement in which the music school gives lessons at schools. We want to retain this given the importance of music in our municipality. Subsidizing music lessons would mean that other educational or cultural hobbies should also receive this.

116938.explanation: We want more police on the street because BOAs do not have the same powers.

116939.explanation: For safety, it is important that sometimes there are images, but the privacy of our residents must also be taken into account. We are not against more cameras, but these may only be used in safety situations.

116940.explanation: Our own citizens deserve priority. Especially our youth, otherwise they will live outside the municipality.

116942.explanation: Because there is already an enormous housing shortage, we do not want to prohibit people from this possibility.

116943.explanation: Polder houses are part of our cultural heritage. They must fit into the environment and hardly disturb the landscape.

116944.explanation: People receiving social assistance who also contribute socially should not be deducted from their benefits for this. If someone can perform full-time work, they do not belong in social assistance. Coercion leads to frustration, not motivation.

116946.explanation: In Hulst, this is currently lower than in other municipalities. We believe that single parents working part-time should not be quickly excluded. This group is often forgotten.

116949.explanation: Where necessary, it is fine, but not as a standard rule.

116950.explanation: No government interference in care choices.

116951.explanation: It is about quality, not financial considerations. Social costs become higher if price is prioritized over quality.

116953.explanation: This has been talked about for too long; it no longer fits the times.

116954.explanation: We want to be an attractive municipality for tourists. We must stimulate tourism. Tourists are very important for, among others, our hospitality sector.

116955.explanation: Establishment for local businesses must be encouraged. No multinationals. Local employment is highly valued. No distribution centers.

116958.explanation: This causes damage to our landscape; there are enough roofs where these can still be placed. Network congestion must first be resolved.

116959.explanation: The swimming pool is an important social facility. It must be preserved. Depending on future developments, it could be decided whether it is financially sensible to keep investing in it.

116961.explanation: It is good to protect our cultural heritage and pass it on to our youth.

116962.explanation: Hulst is a zero-tolerance municipality and we want to maintain that to prevent border issues.

116968.explanation: But as compensation, a large parking and reception area outside the center. We do want short-term parking options in the inner city. Residents of the inner city and their visitors should retain sufficient opportunities.

116970.explanation: Especially given the developments in Waterzande and the expected increase in traffic, I would relieve Kuitaart.

116973.explanation: To preserve the villages and livability, it is important that students have a direct connection to the university city of Breda.

statements.title: There should be a bypass around Kuitaart towards Waterzande.
Hulst should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Hulst should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The industrial estates in Hulst should be allowed to expand.
The residents of Hulst should be able to give their opinion on important topics via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Hulst moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Hulst should stop funding Zwembad Reynaertland.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a new coffeeshop (cannabis coffee shop).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: There should be fewer parking spaces in the center of Hulst.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should enable all primary schools to include the Hulsters dialect in their curriculum.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the province to reinstate a bus to Breda.
